title: Issue Collection (legacy/archive)
description: LEGACY/ARCHIVE form of the {schema_version, issues:[]} wrapper. As of ADR-0011 new issues are one file per record at .excn/issues/<uuid>-<slug>.json (see issue-record.schema.json), so this collection schema is retained ONLY to validate pre-migration backlog.json and the closed sprint-<N>/sprint-<N>-issues.json archive partitions; the per-record schema is authoritative for new writes. An issue's file location is its lifecycle state; the record fields are identical in every partition. IDs are globally unique across all files and depends_on may reference any partition, so validation reads across files, not within one. scribe moves issues between files at sprint boundaries and updates status; descriptions are not rewritten. The widened id/prd patterns mirror UUIDV7_PATTERN in src/bin/write-policy.js.

id.description: Stable identifier, never reused: a legacy id (e.g. PROJ-001) or a UUIDv7.

severity.description: P1 blocking, P2 significant, P3 low-priority.

scope.description: Which area(s) the fix lives in — project-defined.

classification.description: macro = architecture-level; local = tactical; pre-existing = predates current work.

actionable_now.description: false = tracking only, no sprint work planned.

prd.description: The PRD this issue traces to, null if filed directly: a legacy PRD-NNN or a UUIDv7.

acceptance_criteria.description: Verifiable criteria; the issue closes only when all hold.

slice_type.description: HITL = needs human interaction; AFK = completable without it.

depends_on.description: Issue IDs that must close first.
